basics.name: Robert DeLanghe
basics.label: Senior Software Engineer
basics.headline: AI agents ship code. I write the contracts that keep them in check.
basics.summary: Senior engineer at Aura, where I built the controls for shipping AI-written code after unreviewed agent commits reached main. I open-sourced the approach as prx. Agent changes land as merged PRs through one signed flow. Each git write is checked against the capability that holds it.

work.name: Aura
work.summary: On Commerce, the team behind Aura's website, commerce-routing, and business-operations systems. I ship the integration and automation backbone, and built the contract-and-validation layer behind inventory-purchasing.
highlights: Wrote the CLI that replaced Aura's 9-tab spreadsheet supply plan with a single source of truth. It compiles to dozens of downstream schema contracts, each checked by hundreds of test scenarios.

Investigated unreviewed agent-authored commits reaching main. Reconstructed each one's path across five log domains and wrote the post-mortem. Designed the controls that became Aura's policy for shipping AI-written code.

work.name: L2L
work.summary: Owned the quality gates and developer experience for a 20-engineer team: static-analysis CI, a design system, and frontend performance.
highlights: Shipped static-analysis gating into the PR pipeline. It blocks whole defect classes at merge instead of catching them after. Critical bugs fell 20% across the team.

work.name: Pioneer Works
work.summary: Delivered the event-planning platform for a major Brooklyn arts center.
highlights: Derived event status from attached documents, not a human-set flag, across the venue's large concerts.

work.name: Kaleida
work.summary: Automated just-in-time production for an AR and print-making studio.
highlights: Eliminated edition-numbering errors. A database issued serial edition IDs on request, making duplicates impossible by construction.

work.name: Scope of Work
work.summary: Engineered the contact-management system for the manufacturing publication.
highlights: Designed the schema that moved spreadsheet data into a relational database, so a malformed record couldn't be written.

work.name: Recurse Center
work.summary: Type theory in Haskell, using the type system to make invalid states unrepresentable. It's the root of the capability work: push correctness to the boundary, and violations can't compile.
highlights: Completed a selective 12-week programming retreat. Researched how a tight type constraint narrows the space of valid implementations.

projects.name: prx
projects.description: An agent pipeline that drives each work unit to a merged PR. Git-writes are verified against the capability owner that holds them. Evolved from hooksmith and moved to TypeScript when the key contracts proved to be between systems, not machine and kernel.

projects.name: ocap-provenance
projects.description: Provenance extended with object-capability semantics. Each attestation records who produced an artifact and which capabilities they held — and which were denied.

meta._source: Canonical résumé doc in JSON Resume format (jsonresume.org). Authored + signed off here (review.mjs) and published to bdelanghe/site, where build.mjs renders /resume + /resume.json. Curated from a saved LinkedIn webarchive, NOT live-fetched. Site render-context (banner, intro, seeking, nav links, the decorative place line) lives in presentation.json. Edit here, not in site.
